title: QA Director
description: We are seeking an experienced QA Director to lead our quality assurance team and ensure compliance with international medical device regulations including ISO 13485, FDA 21 CFR Part 820, and EU MDR.
responsibilities: Lead and manage the QA team across multiple product lines
Develop and implement quality management systems (QMS)
Ensure compliance with ISO 13485, FDA, and EU MDR requirements
Oversee internal and external audits
Drive continuous improvement initiatives across the organization
Collaborate with R&D, manufacturing, and regulatory teams
Report quality metrics to senior leadership
requirements: 10+ years of experience in quality assurance within the medical device industry
Deep knowledge of ISO 13485, FDA 21 CFR Part 820, and EU MDR
Proven leadership experience managing QA teams
Experience with CAPA, risk management, and design controls
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent communication skills in English; German is a plus

title: Regulatory Affairs Manager
description: Join our regulatory team to manage the submission and approval process for medical devices across European and international markets. You will be the key liaison between our product development teams and regulatory authorities.
responsibilities: Prepare and submit technical documentation for CE marking under EU MDR
Manage 510(k) and PMA submissions to the FDA
Monitor changes in regulatory requirements globally
Support post-market surveillance activities
Coordinate with notified bodies and competent authorities
Train internal teams on regulatory requirements
requirements: 7+ years in regulatory affairs within MedTech or pharma
In-depth knowledge of EU MDR, IVDR, and FDA regulations
Experience with technical file compilation and submissions
RAC certification preferred
Strong project management skills
Fluent in English; additional European languages beneficial
benefits: Modern office in Munich city center

title: Senior Biomedical Engineer
description: We are looking for a Senior Biomedical Engineer to design and develop next-generation implantable medical devices. Join a team pushing the boundaries of medical technology innovation.
responsibilities: Design and develop implantable medical devices
Conduct feasibility studies and prototype testing
Perform design verification and validation activities
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including clinical and regulatory
Create and maintain design history files (DHF)
Apply risk management processes per ISO 14971
requirements: MSc or PhD in Biomedical Engineering or related field
5+ years of medical device development experience
Proficiency in CAD software (SolidWorks, Creo)
Experience with biocompatibility testing and sterilization validation
Knowledge of ISO 13485 and design control processes
Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities
benefits: 25 days vacation plus Swiss public holidays
Continuous learning and PhD sponsorship programs

title: Clinical Data Analyst
description: Support our clinical team by analyzing data from medical device trials and post-market studies. You will play a critical role in generating evidence to support regulatory submissions and product improvements.
responsibilities: Analyze clinical trial data and generate statistical reports
Support clinical evaluation report (CER) preparation
Manage clinical databases and ensure data integrity
Collaborate with clinical investigators and study monitors
Develop data visualization dashboards
Ensure compliance with GCP and MDR clinical requirements
requirements: 3+ years of clinical data analysis experience
Proficiency in statistical software (R, SAS, or Python)
Understanding of clinical trial design and methodology
Knowledge of EU MDR clinical evidence requirements
Experience with EDC systems (Medidata, REDCap)
Strong attention to detail and documentation skills

title: Medical Device Sales Manager
description: Drive revenue growth by managing key accounts and expanding our presence in the DACH medical device market. This role combines strategic sales leadership with hands-on client engagement.
responsibilities: Manage and grow a portfolio of hospital and clinic accounts
Develop and execute regional sales strategies
Conduct product demonstrations and presentations
Negotiate contracts and close high-value deals
Collaborate with marketing on campaign development
Provide market intelligence and competitive analysis
Attend medical conferences and trade shows
requirements: 5+ years of B2B sales experience in MedTech or healthcare
Proven track record of meeting and exceeding sales targets
Strong understanding of hospital procurement processes
Excellent presentation and negotiation skills
Willingness to travel within the DACH region (40%)
Fluent in German and English

title: Software Engineer – Medical Devices
description: Develop embedded software for Class II and Class III medical devices. Work in an agile environment following IEC 62304 lifecycle processes and contribute to life-saving technology.
responsibilities: Develop and maintain embedded software for medical devices
Follow IEC 62304 software development lifecycle
Write unit tests and perform code reviews
Collaborate with hardware and systems engineering teams
Participate in risk analysis and FMEA processes
Create and maintain software documentation
requirements: 4+ years of embedded software development experience
Proficiency in C/C++ and Python
Knowledge of IEC 62304 and IEC 62443 standards
Experience with real-time operating systems (RTOS)
Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and version control (Git)
Understanding of cybersecurity requirements for medical devices
benefits: Competitive Dutch salary with holiday allowance
Modern Amsterdam office with bike parking
